nameMeanings.name: Page
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and French: occupational or status name for a young servant, Middle English page, paige, Old French page (from Italian paggio, ultimately from Greek paidion, a diminutive of pais ‘boy, child’). The surname has also been established in Ireland since the 16th century. In North America, this surname is also a shortened form of the French cognate Lepage .
French Canadian (Pagé): altered form of French Paget , a diminutive of 1. Compare Pashia .
North German: metonymic occupational name for a horse dealer, from Middle Low German page ‘horse’.

variants.name: Pages
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Carlos, Mario, Eduardo, Jorge, Luis, Mauricio, Ramon, Fernando, Jose, Lazaro, Pedro, Alfredo.  French Jacques, Lucien, Michel.

Catalan and southern French (Pagès): occupational name for a peasant farmer or agricultural laborer, from Catalan and Old Occitan pagès ‘peasant’ (from Late Latin pagensis, a derivative of pagus ‘village, country district’).
English: variant of Page with genitival of post-medieval excrescent s.

variants.name: Paget
meanings: French: diminutive of   Page  .
English: variant of   Padgett  .

variants.name: Paige
meanings: English (mainly Devon): variant of   Page  .

variants.name: Pageau
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Stephane, Alphonse, Andre, Fernand, Gisele, Jean Claude, Lucien, Philippe.
French: diminutive of   Page  .

variants.name: Lepage
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Marcel, Andre, Fernand, Pierre, Raoul, Adelard, Alcide, Christophe, Lucien, Normand, Adrien.
French: occupational or status name for a young servant, a variant of Page 1, with fused masculine definite article le. Compare Lapage .
French Canadian: altered form of Pagé (see Page 2).

variants.name: Padgett
meanings: English (mainly Yorkshire): from a Middle English diminutive of page, paige ‘servant, groom’ (see Page ).

variants.name: Littlepage
meanings: English: nickname from Middle English littel ‘small’ +Middle English, Old French page ‘young servant’ (see Page ).

variants.name: Pashia
meanings: Americanized form of French Canadian Pagé (see Page 2).
History: It was Jean Baptiste Pagé from QC whose surname was changed to Pashia after he settled in MO in the 1810s. Some of his descendants bear a rare variant, Pashea.

nameMeanings.name: cousin
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Jacques, Emile, Jeanpaul, Serge.
French: nickname from Old French cusin, cosin ‘first cousin’, from Latin consobrinus, used (also) in the sense ‘friend, crony’. Compare with 2.

English: nickname from Middle English cusin, cosin ‘kinsman or cousin’, a borrowing from Old French (see 1 above). The surname would thus have denoted a person related in some way to a prominent figure in the neighborhood. In some cases it may also have been a nickname for someone who used the term cousin frequently as a familiar term of address.

variants.name: Coussens
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Remi, Prudent, Remy.
Flemish: genitivized altered form of French   Cousin  .
French: possibly an altered form of Coussin: metonymic occupational name for a cushion maker, from Old French coissin ‘cushion’. Alternatively, an altered form of Cousin .

variants.name: Cousins
meanings: English and Irish (Wexford): variant of Cousin , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Cushing
meanings: English (Norfolk): variant of   Cousin  .
Americanized form of French   Cauchon  .

variants.name: Cousineau
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Marcel, Pierre, Monique, Alain, Alcide, Anatole, Andre, Armand, Colombe, Emile, Francoise, Gilles.
French: from a derivative of Old French co(u)sin, cusin ‘cousin’ (see Cousin ). Compare Cousino .

variants.name: Cozine
meanings: Altered form of Dutch Cosijn: relationship name from regional kozijn ‘male cousin’, a word of French origin (see Cousin ). Compare Corzine .

variants.name: Cushen
meanings: English and Irish (southern): variant of   Cousin  . This and   Cussen   are usual forms of the name in Ireland.

en.lifeSummary: cousin Page had at least 2 sons.

fr.lifeSummary: cousin Page a eu au moins 2 fils.

it.lifeSummary: cousin Page lui avuto almeno 2 figli.

ja.lifeSummary: cousin Pageは少なくとも2人の息子をもうけた。

ko.lifeSummary: cousin Page(은)는 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: cousin Page teve pelo menos 2 filhos.

ru.lifeSummary: У cousin Page было по крайней мере 2 сына.

es.lifeSummary: cousin Page tuvo por lo menos 2 hijos.
